fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Connecticut Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Connecticut - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity connecticut, how to transfer audiologist license to connecticut, audiologist compact connecticut, connecticut audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Connecticut Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.mailing_address:
Connecticut Department of Public Health
Practitioner Licensing and Investigations Section
410 Capitol Ave., MS #12PL
P.O. Box 340308
Hartford, CT 06134-0308

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Connecticut.

steps.title: Create an eLicense Account
steps.description: Navigate to the eLicense system at www.elicense.ct.gov and click 'Register a Person' to create a new user account if you do not have one already. You will need to provide personal information and set up login credentials.

steps.title: Start a New Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created or existing eLicense account. From your dashboard, select the option to start a 'New Application' and choose 'Audiologist' as the profession for which you are applying.

steps.title: Complete Application and Upload Documents
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application within the eLicense system. Upload all necessary supporting documents, which typically include official transcripts showing a master's or doctoral degree, verification of supervised professional experience, and proof of passing the national examination in audiology (Praxis).

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee of $200 through the eLicense system. Payment can typically be made using a credit or debit card.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly log in to your eLicense account to check the status of your application. Be prepared to promptly respond to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Connecticut Department of Public Health.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Connecticut audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Connecticut have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in Connecticut. To work in Connecticut, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Connecticut?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Connecticut while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Connecticut
hero.dek: Audiologists seeking initial licensure or licensure by endorsement in Connecticut must apply online through the state's eLicense system.

variants.label: Reciprocity

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to https://www.elicense.ct.gov/, then click 'Register a Person' to create a new account. Follow the prompts to enter your personal information and create login credentials.
